name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.12.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.12.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on December 19, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 580.70 lakh ha (including 87.80 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 91.70 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 572.59 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on December 19, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 580.70 lakh ha (including 87.80 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 91.70 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 572.59 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  on  19.12.2025, 98%  of  area has been  harvested  for Kharif crops all  over India. Approximately,  64%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Uradbean  (100%),  Moongbean  (100%)  & Mothbean (100%); 97.45% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (98.98%),  Soyabean (100%)& Sesamum (97.81%); 96.10% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (97.02%) & Bajra (98.84%) and 93% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.
texts.text:  As  on  19.12.2025, 98%  of  area has been  harvested  for Kharif crops all  over India. Approximately,  64%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Uradbean  (100%),  Moongbean  (100%)  & Mothbean (100%); 97.45% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (98.98%),  Soyabean (100%)& Sesamum (97.81%); 96.10% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (97.02%) & Bajra (98.84%) and 93% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal in North West India, Saurashtra-Kutch, Bihar, Gangetic West Bengal and North East India.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal in North West India, Saurashtra-Kutch, Bihar, Gangetic West Bengal and North East India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  December  21  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  15%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East & Northeast India: 2%; (ii) Northwest India: 35%; (iii) Central India: 37%; and South Peninsula: 6%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  December  21  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  15%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East & Northeast India: 2%; (ii) Northwest India: 35%; (iii) Central India: 37%; and South Peninsula: 6%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Subtropical  westerly  Jet  Stream  with  core  winds  of  the  order  of  120  knots  at  12.6  km above mean sea level continues to prevail over Northwest India.
texts.text:  Subtropical  westerly  Jet  Stream  with  core  winds  of  the  order  of  120  knots  at  12.6  km above mean sea level continues to prevail over Northwest India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Scattered  to  widespread  light/moderate  rainfall/snowfall  very  likely  over  JammuKashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad and isolated to scattered rainfall/snowfall over Himachal Pradesh & Uttarakhand during next 3 days.
texts.text:  Scattered  to  widespread  light/moderate  rainfall/snowfall  very  likely  over  JammuKashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad and isolated to scattered rainfall/snowfall over Himachal Pradesh & Uttarakhand during next 3 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  Thunderstorm,  lightning,  with  Gusty  Wind  likely  to  prevail  over  JammuKashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad on 22nd December.
texts.text:  Isolated  Thunderstorm,  lightning,  with  Gusty  Wind  likely  to  prevail  over  JammuKashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad on 22nd December.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Light  to  moderate  rainfall/Snowfall  accompanied  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  very likely at isolated places over Jammu & Kashmir, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal and over Kerala & Mahe, Lakshadweep and in some days during the week.
texts.text:  Light  to  moderate  rainfall/Snowfall  accompanied  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  very likely at isolated places over Jammu & Kashmir, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal and over Kerala & Mahe, Lakshadweep and in some days during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated to Scattered rainfall activity over Western Himalayan Region, Tamil Nadu and Nicobar Islands.
texts.text:  Isolated to Scattered rainfall activity over Western Himalayan Region, Tamil Nadu and Nicobar Islands.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense fog conditions are likely to prevail during early morning hours in isolated pockets of north India on some days of the week.
texts.text:  Dense fog conditions are likely to prevail during early morning hours in isolated pockets of north India on some days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cold Day conditions very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Uttarakhand, Jharkhand on 22nd; Punjab, Haryana, Bihar and Uttar Pradesh on 22 nd    23 rd  December.
texts.text:  Cold Day conditions very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Uttarakhand, Jharkhand on 22nd; Punjab, Haryana, Bihar and Uttar Pradesh on 22 nd    23 rd  December.

texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  18  December  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  154.438  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  82.5%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 106.80 percent of last  year's storage  and 121.87 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  18  December  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  154.438  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  82.5%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 106.80 percent of last  year's storage  and 121.87 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.
texts.text:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers :. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers :. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (11 Dec - 17 Dec 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 92%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 17 Dec 2025) is higher than LPA by 16%.
texts.text:  During the week (11 Dec - 17 Dec 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 92%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 17 Dec 2025) is higher than LPA by 16%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Minimum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  below  normal  by  2-4°C  over  many  parts  of  the country except northeast India & Western Himalayan Region and many parts of northwest India, where these are likely to be above normal by 1-3°C during the week Gradual rise by 2-3°C over Northwest India during next 3 days and no significant change thereafter.

texts.text:  Wheat: About  301.63  lakh  ha  (96.5%  of  normal)  area  coverage  under  Wheat  has  been reported as compared to 300.34 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.orig:  Rapeseed & Mustard : About 87.80 (111% of normal) lakh ha area coverage under Rapeseed &  Mustard  has  been  reported  as  compared  to  86.57  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rapeseed & Mustard : About 87.80 (111% of normal) lakh ha area coverage under Rapeseed &  Mustard  has  been  reported  as  compared  to  86.57  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding period of last year.
